stories.headline: Apple CEO Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September
stories.summary: Head of hardware to become next chief executive while Cook will become chair of the iPhone maker

stories.headline: ‘Highly unlikely’ Iran ceasefire will be extended if no deal reached, Trump says
stories.summary: US delegation expected to leave ‘soon’ for Pakistan peace talks even as Tehran’s attendance remains unclear

stories.headline: Tim Cook hands Apple over to its hardware guru
stories.summary: John Ternus’s task is to transform the iPhone-maker for the AI era

stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran spurns talks under threats; Trump says blockade stays
stories.summary: Iran says will not accept negotiations 'under the shadow of threats', as Trump promises to continue blockade in Hormuz.

stories.headline: Tim Cook names successor as he steps down as Apple CEO
stories.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a 15-year tenure that saw the company’s market value soar by more than €3.3 trillion during an iPhone-fuelled era of prosperity.

stories.headline: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
stories.summary: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

stories.headline: Iran to send team to Islamabad for US peace talks: Pakistan officials
Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
stories.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

stories.headline: ‘Kiss of death’: How the US killed a Swiss merchant bank
stories.summary: MBaer survived years of scrutiny at home — before Washington stepped in to seal its fate

stories.headline: Babcock’s cheaper warships offer the Royal Navy hope
stories.summary: The UK defence company is building Type 31 frigates quickly at Rosyth to fill an awkward gap

stories.headline: Maga Inc builds nearly $350mn war chest ahead of midterm elections
stories.summary: Pro-Trump Super Pac raises $35mn in March with donations from billionaire Diane Hendricks and Andreessen Horowitz

stories.headline: Poland Fines Luxury Car Trader for Illegal Exports to Russia
stories.summary: Poland has fined a local company 20 million zloty ($5.5 million) for intentionally violating European Union sanctions by selling luxury cars to Russia, the country’s tax authority said.

stories.headline: Wall Street Banks Flag Risks for Kenya’s Shilling as War Rages On
stories.summary: Kenya’s shilling is among the most vulnerable currencies in Africa, according to strategists at major banks, who predict further weakness as elevated oil prices pile pressure on the economy.

stories.headline: AI Boom Propels Kospi to Record as Tech Giants Defy War Jitters
stories.summary: Investor optimism that the worst of the Iran war may be over was on full display Tuesday, when the return of the AI trade drove South Korean stocks to a record and spurred another rally in Taiwan.

stories.headline: Apple Names New CEO; Korean Stocks Notch New High | The Asia Trade 4/21/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump’s Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer latest to leave administration
stories.summary: Chavez-DeRemer leaves the Trump administration after the recent departures of Kristi Noem and Pam Bondi.

stories.headline: Hormuz crisis delivers a Taiwan Strait wake-up call: 5 things to know
US Labour Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaves Trump administration after allegations of misconduct
stories.summary: Trump’s labour secretary becomes the third cabinet member to leave the administration in less than two months. Earlier in March, the US president fired Homeland Secretary Kristi Noem and soon after dismissed Attorney General Pam Bondi.

stories.headline: AB Foods to Separate Its Budget Apparel Chain Primark
stories.summary: Associated British Foods Plc will separate budget apparel chain Primark from the rest of its business, in a breakup of one of the UK’s biggest conglomerates.

stories.headline: Pakistan’s Bid to Mediate US‑Iran Talks Amid Uncertainty
stories.summary:
Pakistan is positioning itself as a potential mediator between the United States and Iran, with officials preparing to host a possible second round of talks despite lingering uncertainty. The effort highlights Islamabad’s diplomatic outreach at a time of heightened regional tensions and fragile US‑Iran engagement.
The discussions matter as any renewed dialogue could ease pressure in the Middle East and shape regional security calculations for Sou

stories.headline: Data-Center Cooling Stocks Sink in China on Competition Concerns
stories.summary: Shares of Chinese liquid-cooling providers fell after a closely watched company reported earnings that missed expectations, sparking concerns over intensifying competition.

stories.headline: Chinese Manufacturing Growth Overtaken by Finance Amid IPO Boom
stories.summary: Chinese manufacturing grew slower than finance for the first time in years, turbocharged by capital raised through share sales during a quarter that saw a surprise rebound in the economy.

stories.headline: EQT Raises $15.6B for Asia Private Equity Fund
stories.summary:
Jean Eric Salata, Chairman at EQT Asia, discusses how the firm raised $15.6 billion for its latest private equity Asia fund, as global investors look beyond the US amid elevated uncertainty. He speaks with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. 

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China’s Wanhua Bets on Overseas Growth as Trade Risks Mount
stories.summary: China’s Wanhua Chemical Group Co. pledged to accelerate its overseas expansion to help counter rising trade risks after the petrochemical giant reported an increase in first-quarter earnings.

stories.headline: Woolworths engaged in ‘marketing magic’ to trick customers, consumer watchdog tells court
stories.summary: The trial between the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission and the supermarket giant began in the federal court in Sydney on TuesdayFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastWoolworths engaged in “marketing magic” to trick customers into thinking they were getting genuine discounts as part of the supermarket’s “Prices Dropped” promotion, the consumer regulator has to

stories.headline: Algeria’s ex-minister of industry jailed in high-profile corruption case
stories.summary: Former minister among several people jailed in corruption case that saw prison sentences of between three and 10 years.

stories.headline: Asia's clean power transition accelerates fall in global fossil-fuel use
Scroll, like, repeat: how the EU plans to boost children's protection online
stories.summary: The Commission laid out its plan to protect minors online as it presents a new age-verification system. Member states are legislating to ban children from social media, while the European Parliament is calling for a strict 16-year limit.

stories.headline: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO
stories.summary: Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on September 1.

stories.headline: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.summary: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.headline: Trump says he will not lift blockade on Iranian ports until peace deal struck with Tehran
stories.summary: Trump’s announcement comes after Iran said earlier it would not be sending a delegation to participate in the second round of high-stakes peace talks in Islamabad that were set to take place on Monday.

stories.headline: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

stories.headline: What we know about the Iranian ship seized by the US
stories.summary: The US has intercepted an Iranian ship entering the Gulf as part of its naval blockade, US President Donald Trump has said.

stories.headline: Oil Slips as Iran Set to Attend Negotiations With US in Pakistan
stories.summary: Oil slipped on signs that Iran will attend negotiations with the US in Islamabad before a ceasefire between the sides ends.

stories.headline: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
stories.summary: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.

stories.headline: House Ethics Panel Defends Handling of Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: In urging victims to step forward, the secretive panel in charge of investigating charges of misconduct by members of Congress acknowledged limits that could allow offenses to go unaddressed.

stories.headline: Japan tsunami alerts downgraded following powerful earthquake off northern coast – as it happened
stories.summary: People in affected areas are still urged to evacuate after quake registering 7.7 magnitude Australian officials in Japan are urgently following up on the tsunami warning off the northeastern coast of the island of Honshu.The Australian government said:We stand ready to provide consular assistance.Australians in need of emergency consular assistance should contact the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade’s 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on 1

stories.headline: EU eyes Ukraine loan, Israeli settler sanctions, after Hungarian election
stories.summary: Ouster of EU 'spoiler-in-chief' Viktor Orban unlocks key foreign policy decisions in the 27-member bloc.

stories.headline: What to know about the next round of US-Iran talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US and Iran are supposed to have another round of direct talks in Pakistan on Tuesday, but will the Iranians skip?

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

stories.headline: Global Stock Rally Resumes, Oil Dips on Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally in global equities resumed after a brief pause, as signs Iran may join talks with the US added to optimism over progress in the Middle East ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline. Oil declined.

stories.headline: Indonesia Stocks Slide After MSCI Delays High-Stakes Review
stories.summary: Indonesian equities slid after MSCI Inc. extended its market status review to June to assess regulatory reforms and warned it would remove major stocks with tightly-held ownership from its indexes.

stories.headline: Kansas Mayor Who Voted Illegally Will Not Serve Time in Prison
stories.summary: Joe Ceballos, a green card holder who resigned as Coldwater’s mayor after being charged, said he did not know that he had to be a citizen to vote.

stories.headline: Kevin Warsh, Trump’s Fed Pick, to Defend Bank’s Ability to Set Rates Independently
stories.summary: Kevin M. Warsh is set to testify at the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday even as a criminal investigation into the central bank risks delaying his ascension to become the next chair.

stories.headline: House Ethics panel makes rare request for information on sexual misconduct
stories.summary: The rare move comes after recent scandals raised questions from lawmakers and others about how Congress handles investigations involving its members.

stories.headline: Carney says Canada’s strong economic ties to US are ‘weakness’ to be corrected
stories.summary: Prime minister details efforts to attract investment and sign trade deals with other countries in 10-minute video addressCanada’s strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, the country’s prime minister has warned.In a 10-minute video address, Mark Carney spoke about his government’s efforts to strengthen the Canadian economy by attracting new investments and signing trade deals wi

stories.headline: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
stories.summary: Former president’s win could end years of weak coalition governments but critics say it may be bad for EUBulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues.With all votes counted on Monday, the Progressive Bulgaria party of Rumen Radev, a former fighter pilot and air

stories.headline: Watch: Magyar visits Tusk - will there be a Warsaw on the Danube?
stories.summary: Péter Magyar has made his first diplomatic priority clear. Before Brussels or Vienna, Hungary's prime minister-elect is heading to Warsaw to meet Donald Tusk. What will they discuss?

stories.headline: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
stories.summary: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

stories.headline: Rebel Wilson's claims against actress are 'malicious concoctions', Australian court hears
stories.summary: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

stories.headline: Fitch Cuts Philippines’ Rating Outlook as Growth Prospects Dim
stories.summary: Fitch Ratings revised its outlook on the Philippines’ credit rating to negative from stable, saying a decline in public investment and rising energy costs have created risks to the Southeast Asian nation’s economic growth.

stories.headline: Democrats Once Loathed Gerrymandering. Now They’re Pushing for It.
stories.summary: The party is seeking an extra edge in Virginia at a time when its thinking has changed on the partisan drawing of political maps — in large part, leaders say, because of President Trump’s tactics.

stories.headline: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
stories.summary: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be setThe arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew.Four Gen Z activists, Herizo Andriamanantena, Miora Rakotomalala, Dina Randrianarisoa and Nomena Ratsihorimanana,

stories.headline: Slovakia to vote in July referendum on scrapping lifelong payments for politicians
stories.summary: Slovak prime ministers and parliament speakers who served at least two terms in office receive a lifelong payment, a monthly sum that equals the salaries of lawmakers in Parliament.

stories.headline: Crime rates in Germany are falling but sexual offences are significantly on the rise
stories.summary: The statistics only cover police investigations so no conclusions can be drawn about convictions or the actual number of offenders. The study also does not include cases that are not reported.

stories.headline: FBI director Kash Patel files $250ml defamation suit against The Atlantic
stories.summary: According to The Atlantic, Patel's job as head of the Federal Bureau of Investigation is in jeopardy in part because of what it said were "bouts of excessive drinking" and "unexplained absences."

stories.headline: 'If Druzhba is operational, Zelenskyy should open it,' Magyar says about pipeline
stories.summary: "He should not go down that street," incoming Hungarian PM Péter Magyar said when asked about new potential Ukrainian demands to reopen the Druzhba pipeline. Brussels ready for Hungary to lift veto if oil flows resume.

stories.headline: Almost 43,000 migrants register in first three days of Spain's regularisation amnesty
stories.summary: Spain's position sharply differs from prevailing attitudes on immigration in Europe, where many governments have been trying to curb arrivals and step up deportations.

stories.headline: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
stories.summary: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
stories.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

stories.headline: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
stories.summary: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

stories.headline: Australia's most-decorated soldier vows to fight war crime charges
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith has given his first statement since he was charged with five counts of the war crime of murder last week.

stories.headline: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
stories.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
stories.summary: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

stories.headline: Drone footage shows huge Malaysian coastal village fire
stories.summary: Thousands of people have been displaced after a fire destroyed around 1,000 homes in Malaysia's Sabah state.

stories.headline: Watch: Runners v robots at China half marathon
stories.summary: Robots competed in a half marathon race in Beijing on Sunday, with the winning machine leaving its human rivals for dust.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
stories.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

stories.headline: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.headline: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: He made jazz under air raids - and built an Indian city's music scene
stories.summary: War correspondent, jazz bandleader and impresario, KC Sen shaped Kolkata’s music scene.

stories.headline: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.headline: The desperate pursuit of final approval
stories.summary: An office parable

stories.headline: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.
